questions.text: What is the primary configuration file for a Hardhat project?

options.text: package.json
hardhat.config.js
truffle-config.js
hardhat.config.sol

questions.explanation: hardhat.config.js is the main configuration file where you define Solidity compiler settings, networks, and plugins. It's a JavaScript file that exports a configuration object.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: B

hardhat.config.js is the main configuration file where you define Solidity compiler settings, networks, and plugins. It's a JavaScript file that exports a configuration object.

questions.text: Which command compiles all Solidity contracts in a Hardhat project?

options.text: npx hardhat test
npx hardhat compile
npx hardhat deploy
npx hardhat run scripts/compile.js

questions.explanation: npx hardhat compile invokes the Solidity compiler, generates bytecode and ABI artifacts, and caches results for incremental builds.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: B

npx hardhat compile invokes the Solidity compiler, generates bytecode and ABI artifacts, and caches results for incremental builds.

questions.text: What unique feature does Hardhat Network provide that is NOT available on live blockchain nodes?

options.text: The ability to use console.log directly in Solidity code.
The ability to send transactions.
The ability to deploy smart contracts.
The ability to query block numbers.

questions.explanation: Hardhat Network intercepts console calls at the EVM level and prints output to the terminal. This works only on Hardhat Network, not on live chains. All other options are standard on any node.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: A

Hardhat Network intercepts console calls at the EVM level and prints output to the terminal. This works only on Hardhat Network, not on live chains. All other options are standard on any node.

questions.text: In hardhat.config.js, how do you define the MST Testnet for deployment?

options.text: By adding an object with url and chainId inside networks.
By creating a separate mst.config.js file.
By setting an environment variable MST_RPC_URL and nothing else.
By importing a MST plugin that auto-configures the network.

questions.explanation: Networks are defined as properties under module.exports.networks. Each network requires at least a url (RPC endpoint) and a chainId. Accounts are provided via the accounts array.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: A

Networks are defined as properties under module.exports.networks. Each network requires at least a url (RPC endpoint) and a chainId. Accounts are provided via the accounts array.

questions.text: What is the primary benefit of Hardhat's plugin-based architecture?

options.text: It allows you to include only the features you need (gas reporting, verification, etc.) without bloating the core framework.
It makes Hardhat compatible with all operating systems.
It enables Hardhat to run directly in a web browser.
It automatically writes tests for your contracts.

questions.explanation: Plugins keep the core lightweight and allow the community to extend Hardhat with new features. You require only the plugins you need for your project.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: A

Plugins keep the core lightweight and allow the community to extend Hardhat with new features. You require only the plugins you need for your project.
